key_points.text: Exceptional revenue growth of 65% year-over-year for fiscal 2026, with total revenue of $215.9 billion versus $130.5 billion in fiscal 2025, driven primarily by accelerated computing and AI solutions in the Data Center segment.
Data Center computing revenue grew 59% driven by demand for Blackwell computing platform, while Data Center networking grew 142% driven by NVLink compute fabric and Ethernet/InfiniBand platforms.

citation.quote: Revenue from Data Center computing grew 59% driven by demand for our Blackwell computing platform. Revenue from Data Center networking grew 142% driven by the introduction and continued ramp of NVLink compute fabric for GB200 and GB300 systems and the growth of Ethernet and InfiniBand platforms.

key_points.text: Strong gross margin improvement to 74.9% in Q1 FY2027, up from 60.5% a year ago, due to non-recurrence of the prior year's $4.5 billion H20 inventory charge and favorable product mix.

citation.quote: Gross margin increased from a year ago on lower inventory provisions, primarily due to the prior year's $4.5 billion charge associated with H20 excess inventory and purchase obligations.

key_points.text: Q1 FY2027 total revenue of $81.6 billion, up 85% year-over-year, with Compute Networking revenue growing 88% to $74.6 billion driven by Blackwell systems and networking solutions ramp.

Outstanding long-term shareholder value demonstrated by stock returning 1,348.75% on $100 invested on January 31, 2021, compared to S&P 500's 100.33% and Nasdaq 100's 106.01% over the five-year period.

Compute Networking segment operating income grew 142% year-over-year in Q1 FY2027 to $53.3 billion from $22.1 billion, driven by strong revenue growth and the one-year prior non-recurrence of the H20 charge.
Edge Computing revenue of $6.4 billion in Q1 FY2027, up 29% year-over-year and up 10% sequentially, driven by robust Blackwell workstation demand.

citation.quote: Edge Computing revenue for the first quarter was $6.4 billion, up 29% from a year ago and up 10% sequentially. The increases were driven by robust Blackwell workstation demand

key_points.text: Dividend increased 25x from $0.01 to $0.25 per share on May 18, 2026, reflecting management confidence in the company's cash generation and financial strength.

citation.quote: On May 18, 2026, we increased our quarterly cash dividend from $ 0.01 per share to $ 0.25 per share to all shareholders of record on June 4, 2026.
